subject: Import data type in UI
commit_message:
Import data type in UI

Develop all necessary changes in the UI to allow importing a data type from a yaml file

Issue-ID: SDC-4279
Signed-off-by: franciscovila <javier.paradela.vila@est.tech>
Change-Id: Id413386fad8b362e8c4a1d25c859a22178189074

Patch Set 12:

With this change it is no longer possible to add properties of data types - open any data type from the catalog, select the Properties view - the properties are shown as greyed out (even though it is still possible to select them) and the "+ Add Property" is missing

Patch Set 13:

Importing to a model other than ONAP SDC has the following issues:

1. When you select the model, the "Derived From" field is reset forcing the user to re-enter the "Derived From" even when the already shown "Derived From" value is valid for the selected model 
2. It would be better if the list of models shown were only those for which the derived from type is valid (I dont mind if this is done separately as an improvement)
3. A ClassCastException is thrown by final Map<String, Object> typesYamlMap = new Yaml().load(typesYaml) in ModelOperation::addTypesToDefaultImports. This can be overcome by using loadAsMap instead
4. When 3. above is addressed the yaml added to the additional_type_definitions.yaml in a service csar for the model gives an overly verbose representation of the added data type. In other use cases where we import data types this is not a problem as we take the data type definition string as it is from the file contents. However here we are using snakeyaml to dump it from the DataTypeDefinitionDto. I can see two possible solutions here: A) if we take a string rather than DTO in the API then this problem goes away. B) For node types and templates we have a lot of logic to ensure only what is needed is added in the generated output (see ToscaExportHandler::exportComponent())  - if we continue with the Dto approach then we may need to do something similar to this for data types
5. Also in the additional_type_definitions.yaml the name of the data type appears to be missing

Patch Set 14:

> Patch Set 13:
> 
> Importing to a model other than ONAP SDC has the following issues:
> 
> 1. When you select the model, the "Derived From" field is reset forcing the user to re-enter the "Derived From" even when the already shown "Derived From" value is valid for the selected model 
> 2. It would be better if the list of models shown were only those for which the derived from type is valid (I dont mind if this is done separately as an improvement)
> 3. A ClassCastException is thrown by final Map<String, Object> typesYamlMap = new Yaml().load(typesYaml) in ModelOperation::addTypesToDefaultImports. This can be overcome by using loadAsMap instead
> 4. When 3. above is addressed the yaml added to the additional_type_definitions.yaml in a service csar for the model gives an overly verbose representation of the added data type. In other use cases where we import data types this is not a problem as we take the data type definition string as it is from the file contents. However here we are using snakeyaml to dump it from the DataTypeDefinitionDto. I can see two possible solutions here: A) if we take a string rather than DTO in the API then this problem goes away. B) For node types and templates we have a lot of logic to ensure only what is needed is added in the generated output (see ToscaExportHandler::exportComponent())  - if we continue with the Dto approach then we may need to do something similar to this for data types
> 5. Also in the additional_type_definitions.yaml the name of the data type appears to be missing

DONE
